name: Black Bean-Goat Cheese Enchiladas (with Mango
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
To make the sauce, in a saucepan, cook the chicken stock, tomatillos, garlic cloves, onions, and serranos over medium-high heat for 10 minutes, stirring frequently. Transfer the mixture to a blender, add the cilantro, and puree until smooth. Season with salt and set aside.
To make the filling, in another saucepan, bring all the filling ingredients except the cheese and salt to a boil. Remove from the heat, whisk in 4 ounces of goat cheese, and season with salt. Heat the oil in a skillet until it reaches 350°F or begins to smoke. (CAUTION: Handle this very carefully – it is extremely hot.) Dip each tortilla into the oil for about 5 seconds on each side, then drain on paper towels and keep warm; do not stack the tortillas.
To assemble the enchiladas, evenly distribute the black bean-goat cheese mixture among the tortillas, spreading down the middle. Roll up the tortillas seam-side down onto a baking sheet or an oven-safe dish, placing them snugly together. Pour the reserved tomatillo sauce over the enchiladas and top with the remaining 4 ounces of goat cheese. Cover with foil and bake in the oven for 10 minutes.
Serve 2 enchiladas per plate, accompanied by Mango Relish (separate recipe).
